content.content:
100	
101	**Pitfalls:**
102	- If a clip is trimmed to its absolute start or end (no handle), Kdenlive will freeze the last available frame to fill the transition — visible as a stutter. Always capture a few extra seconds on each end of every shot.
103	- Mixes shift clip timing. If you are working to a music beat, add the mix before locking to the beat, or adjust after.
104	
105	**Performance:** Lightweight. Renders in real time on most hardware.
106	
107	---
108	
109	### Composite Transitions (Overlap Method)
110	
111	**Definition:** Two clips placed on separate tracks with overlapping time ranges. The overlap region is governed by a **composition** (Kdenlive's term for a two-track transition). This is the classic NLE transition workflow.
112	
113	**Use cases:**
114	- Any transition that needs fine control over duration, position, and blending
115	- Picture-in-picture, split screens, and layered compositing
116	- When you need keyframed opacity or movement during the transition
117	
118	**How-to:**
119	
120	1. Place clip A on track V1 and clip B on track V2, overlapping by the desired transition duration.
121	2. Look for the small purple triangle/dot in the corner of the overlap zone on the upper clip.
122	3. Click the purple dot to create a composition, or right-click the overlap and select `Insert a composition → Wipe` (or Dissolve, Slide, etc.).
123	4. Adjust parameters in the Effect Stack.
124	
125	**Pitfalls:**
126	- Track order matters. The upper track is composited **over** the lower track. If the transition looks backward, swap which track holds which clip.
127	- Forgetting to add a composition to the overlap means the upper clip simply covers the lower clip with no blending.
128	
129	**Performance:** Moderate. Two video streams decoded simultaneously. Use proxies for 4K+ footage.
130	
131	---
132	
133	### Wipe and Wipe Methods
134	
135	**Definition:** A transition where one image progressively replaces another along a boundary defined by a **luma map** (grayscale gradient image). The boundary can be a straight line, circle, star, clock sweep, or any arbitrary shape.
136	
137	**Use cases:**
138	- Scene changes with a strong directional feel (left-to-right, top-to-bottom)
139	- Stylized reveals (iris wipe, star wipe, clock wipe)
140	- Custom branded transitions using hand-made `.pgm` luma files
141	
142	**How-to:**
143	
144	1. Create a two-track overlap (see Composite Transitions above).
145	2. Add a `Wipe` composition to the overlap zone.
146	3. In the Effect Stack, choose a wipe method (built-in options include bar, barn door, clock, iris, box, and many more).
147	4. Adjust **softness** to control the feathered edge width (0 = hard line, higher = softer gradient).
148	5. Use **Invert** to reverse the wipe direction. Use **Reverse** to swap which clip is revealed vs. hidden.
149	
150	**Custom luma maps:**
151	- Create a grayscale `.pgm` image (Portable GrayMap) the same resolution as your project.
152	- Black regions transition first; white regions transition last; gray regions transition proportionally.
153	- Place the file in `~/.local/share/kdenlive/lumas/HD/` (or the corresponding resolution folder).
154	- Select it from the wipe method dropdown.
155	
156	**Pitfalls:**
157	- Low-softness wipes on compressed footage can show banding artifacts. Increase softness to 10--20% to hide them.
158	- Custom `.pgm` files must be the correct resolution or Kdenlive will stretch them, distorting the wipe shape.
159	
160	**Performance:** Low to moderate. The luma map is a single grayscale image; the GPU handles blending quickly.
161	
162	> **ForgeFrame:** Use `/ff-composite-wipe` to apply a branded wipe transition with your project's luma map:
163	>
164	> ```
165	> /ff-composite-wipe
166	> Input: clip_a="screen-recording.mp4", clip_b="demo-step-2.mp4",
167	>        method="bar", direction="left", softness=15, duration_frames=18
168	> Output: timeline updated with Wipe composition at cut point
169	> ```
170	>
171	> You can also do this manually: create a two-track overlap, add a Wipe composition, choose "bar" from the method dropdown, and set softness to 15 in the Effect Stack.
172	
173	---
174	
175	### Dissolve and Luma Dissolve
176	
177	**Definition:** A **dissolve** (also called a cross-dissolve) gradually blends the opacity of two overlapping clips so that clip A fades out while clip B fades in. A **luma dissolve** uses a grayscale map to control which parts of the frame dissolve first, combining the dissolve concept with the spatial control of a wipe.
178	
179	**Use cases:**
180	- Dissolve: passage of time, dream sequences, gentle scene changes
181	- Luma dissolve: organic or textured transitions (using noise, smoke, or paint-stroke luma maps)
182	
183	**How-to:**
184	
185	1. Create a two-track overlap.
186	2. Add a `Dissolve` composition for a standard cross-dissolve.
187	3. For a luma dissolve, add a `Wipe` composition and set softness very high (80--100%). This blurs the boundary so much that it behaves like a dissolve with spatial variation.
188	
189	**Pitfalls:**
190	- Long dissolves (> 2 seconds) can look muddy if both clips have high visual complexity. Keep dissolves short or use them when one clip is relatively simple (e.g., a solid color or a soft-focus shot).
191	- A dissolve into or out of black is a **fade**, not a dissolve. Use the `Fade in` / `Fade out` effects on a single clip for that.
192	
193	**Performance:** Low. Simple alpha blending.
194	
195	---
196	
197	### Slide
198	
199	**Definition:** One clip physically moves off-screen (or on-screen) to reveal the clip beneath it. Unlike a wipe, both images are fully visible during the transition — one is sliding over or away from the other.
200	
201	**Use cases:**
202	- Energetic, editorial-style transitions
203	- Revealing a new topic or segment
204	- Split-screen animations where panels slide into position
205	
206	**How-to:**
207	
208	1. Create a two-track overlap.
209	2. Add a `Slide` composition.
210	3. Choose the slide direction (left, right, up, down).
211	4. Adjust duration to taste. Fast slides (8--12 frames) feel punchy; slow slides (1--2 seconds) feel cinematic.
212	
213	**Pitfalls:**
214	- Slides that are too slow look like a software glitch rather than a creative choice. Err on the side of faster.
215	- Combining a slide with a dissolve requires stacking two compositions or using keyframed Transform effects instead.
216	
217	**Performance:** Low. Just repositioning decoded frames.
218	
219	---
220	
221	## Picture-in-Picture and Compositing
222	
223	### Composite (General Compositing Tool)
224	
225	**Definition:** The `Composite` transition type in Kdenlive layers two clips using configurable opacity and geometry. Unlike the specialized transitions above, Composite is a general-purpose compositing tool that you control entirely through keyframes.
226	
227	**Use cases:**
228	- Picture-in-picture (PiP) layouts
229	- Gradual opacity fades between layers with precise keyframe control
230	- Any layered effect where you need to animate position, scale, and opacity simultaneously
231	
232	**How-to:**
233	
234	1. Place clips on two tracks with overlap.
235	2. Add a `Composite` composition.
236	3. In the Effect Stack, set **opacity** (0--100%) and adjust the **geometry** rectangle to position and scale the upper clip.
237	4. Enable keyframing on opacity and geometry to animate the composite over time.
238	5. Adjust **softness** for feathered edges on the geometry boundary.
239	
240	**Pitfalls:**
241	- Composite does not include blending modes (multiply, screen, etc.). For those, use the `Cairo Blend` or `Cairographics Blend` effect on the upper clip instead.
242	- Keyframing both geometry and opacity simultaneously can produce unexpected results if keyframe positions do not align. Add keyframes at the same timestamps for both parameters.
243	
244	**Performance:** Moderate. Keyframed composites with softness at high resolution benefit from proxy editing.
245	
246	---
247	
248	### PiP Layouts
249	
250	Picture-in-picture (PiP) is one of the most useful compositing tools for tutorial creators. A common use case: show your talking head in the corner while the screen recording fills the frame.
251	
252	**Standard PiP layout for tutorials:**
253	
254	| Layout | Upper clip position | Scale | Notes |
255	|---|---|---|---|
256	| Bottom-right corner | X: 70%, Y: 65% | 30% | Most common for talking head overlays |
257	| Bottom-left corner | X: 2%, Y: 65% | 30% | Use when screen action is in the bottom-right |
258	| Top-right corner | X: 70%, Y: 2% | 30% | Use when screen action is in the lower portion |
259	| Split screen (50/50) | Left: X: 0%, Y: 0% — Right: X: 50%, Y: 0% | 50% each | Comparison or side-by-side tutorial steps |
260	
261	**How-to:**
262	
263	1. Place the main clip (screen recording) on V1.
264	2. Place the overlay clip (talking head) on V2 with the same time range.
265	3. Add a `Composite` or `Composite & Transform` composition to the V2 clip.
266	4. In the Effect Stack, set the geometry to your desired position and scale.
267	5. Keyframe the geometry to animate the PiP in/out if needed (e.g., slide in from off-screen at the start of a segment).
268	
269	> **ForgeFrame:** Use `/ff-composite-pip` to place a PiP overlay automatically:
270	>
271	> ```
272	> /ff-composite-pip
273	> Input: main="screen-recording.mp4", overlay="talking-head.mp4",
274	>        position="bottom-right", scale=0.30, fade_in=true
275	> Output: timeline updated with Composite composition and keyframed geometry
276	> ```
277	>
278	> You can also do this manually: place the overlay clip on V2, add a Composite composition, and set the geometry rectangle to 30% scale in the Effect Stack.
279	
280	---
281	
282	## When NOT to Use Transitions
283	
284	This is the section most tutorials skip.
285	
286	**The default should always be a hard cut.** Every other transition choice is a deliberate departure from that default — and departures need justification.
287	
288	### The questions to ask before adding a transition
289	
290	1. **Does this serve the narrative?** A dissolve can signal the passage of time. A wipe can suggest geographic change. A slide can reinforce a "moving forward" theme. If you cannot articulate why a transition fits the story at this specific moment, use a cut.
291	
292	2. **Am I hiding a problem?** Transitions are often added to disguise awkward cuts, jump cuts, or mismatched audio. Fix the underlying problem instead. A dissolve over a jump cut usually looks worse than the jump cut itself.
293	
294	3. **Is this consistent with my edit so far?** One wipe in a video of hard cuts draws massive attention to itself. Either commit to a style throughout a sequence or stay with cuts.
295	
296	4. **Am I compensating for missing B-roll?** If a transition feels necessary because a static talking-head cut feels boring, the answer is more B-roll, not a dissolve.
297	
298	### Transitions that almost always signal a mistake
299	
300	| Transition | The problem it usually signals |
301	|---|---|
302	| Slow dissolve between two similar shots | Jump cut disguise — use a cutaway instead |
303	| Wipe in a corporate tutorial | Draws attention to itself; rarely serves the content |
304	| Star wipe, clock wipe | Almost never appropriate in non-comedic content |
305	| Any transition applied uniformly to every cut | The editor hasn't thought about each cut individually |
306	| Dissolve at every scene change | Passage-of-time effect loses meaning through repetition |
307	
308	### When transitions genuinely work
309	
310	- **Dissolve:** Time lapse, dream sequences, memory sequences, end-of-section soft landings before a chapter card
311	- **Wipe:** Reveal moments where you want the viewer to notice the transition (deliberately stylized)
312	- **Slide:** High-energy sequences, product reveals, or branded transitions where motion graphics are part of the identity
313	- **Fade to black / from black:** Opening and closing segments; separating major structural sections of a long video
314	- **J/L cuts:** Always. Use these by default for every conversational cut.
315	
316	> [!tip] Restraint is craft
317	> Tutorial creators who use hard cuts and J/L cuts exclusively often produce cleaner, faster-paced content than creators who reach for transitions as decoration. Train yourself to cut first, then ask if a transition adds anything.
318	
319	---
320	
321	## Quick Reference
322	
323	### Transition Type Selector
324	
325	| You want to... | Use this |
326	|---|---|
327	| Connect two clips invisibly | Hard cut + J/L cut for audio |
328	| Show passage of time | Short dissolve (0.5--1 sec) |
329	| Wipe to reveal new content | Wipe composition (bar or iris) |
330	| Layer a talking head over screen recording | PiP with Composite composition |
331	| Animate text or graphics entering the frame | Slide composition or Transform + keyframes |
332	| Fade to black between sections | Fade out / Fade in on single clips |
333	
334	### Composition Type Reference
335	
336	| Composition | Best for | Track setup needed |
337	|---|---|---|
338	| Mix (same-track) | Simple dissolves between cuts | Same track |
339	| Dissolve | Cross-fades with minimal control | Two-track overlap |
340	| Wipe | Directional or shaped reveals | Two-track overlap |
341	| Slide | Movement-based transitions | Two-track overlap |
342	| Composite | PiP, opacity, animated geometry | Two-track overlap |
343

> ```
>
> You can also do this manually: create a two-track overlap, add a Wipe composition, choose "bar" from the method dropdown, and set softness to 15 in the Effect Stack.

---

### Dissolve and Luma Dissolve

**Definition:** A **dissolve** (also called a cross-dissolve) gradually blends the opacity of two overlapping clips so that clip A fades out while clip B fades in. A **luma dissolve** uses a grayscale map to control which parts of the frame dissolve first, combining the dissolve concept with the spatial control of a wipe.

**Use cases:**
- Dissolve: passage of time, dream sequences, gentle scene changes
- Luma dissolve: organic or textured transitions (using noise, smoke, or paint-stroke luma maps)

**How-to:**

1. Create a two-track overlap.
2. Add a `Dissolve` composition for a standard cross-dissolve.
3. For a luma dissolve, add a `Wipe` composition and set softness very high (80--100%). This blurs the boundary so much that it behaves like a dissolve with spatial variation.

**Pitfalls:**
- Long dissolves (> 2 seconds) can look muddy if both clips have high visual complexity. Keep dissolves short or use them when one clip is relatively simple (e.g., a solid color or a soft-focus shot).
- A dissolve into or out of black is a **fade**, not a dissolve. Use the `Fade in` / `Fade out` effects on a single clip for that.

**Performance:** Low. Simple alpha blending.

---

### Slide

**Definition:** One clip physically moves off-screen (or on-screen) to reveal the clip beneath it. Unlike a wipe, both images are fully visible during the transition — one is sliding over or away from the other.

**Use cases:**
- Energetic, editorial-style transitions
- Revealing a new topic or segment
- Split-screen animations where panels slide into position

**How-to:**

1. Create a two-track overlap.
2. Add a `Slide` composition.
3. Choose the slide direction (left, right, up, down).
4. Adjust duration to taste. Fast slides (8--12 frames) feel punchy; slow slides (1--2 seconds) feel cinematic.

**Pitfalls:**
- Slides that are too slow look like a software glitch rather than a creative choice. Err on the side of faster.
- Combining a slide with a dissolve requires stacking two compositions or using keyframed Transform effects instead.

**Performance:** Low. Just repositioning decoded frames.

---

## Picture-in-Picture and Compositing

### Composite (General Compositing Tool)

**Definition:** The `Composite` transition type in Kdenlive layers two clips using configurable opacity and geometry. Unlike the specialized transitions above, Composite is a general-purpose compositing tool that you control entirely through keyframes.

**Use cases:**
- Picture-in-picture (PiP) layouts
- Gradual opacity fades between layers with precise keyframe control
- Any layered effect where you need to animate position, scale, and opacity simultaneously

**How-to:**

1. Place clips on two tracks with overlap.
2. Add a `Composite` composition.
3. In the Effect Stack, set **opacity** (0--100%) and adjust the **geometry** rectangle to position and scale the upper clip.
4. Enable keyframing on opacity and geometry to animate the composite over time.
5. Adjust **softness** for feathered edges on the geometry boundary.

**Pitfalls:**
- Composite does not include blending modes (multiply, screen, etc.). For those, use the `Cairo Blend` or `Cairographics Blend` effect on the upper clip instead.
- Keyframing both geometry and opacity simultaneously can produce unexpected results if keyframe positions do not align. Add keyframes at the same timestamps for both parameters.

**Performance:** Moderate. Keyframed composites with softness at high resolution benefit from proxy editing.

---

### PiP Layouts

Picture-in-picture (PiP) is one of the most useful compositing tools for tutorial creators. A common use case: show your talking head in the corner while the screen recording fills the frame.

**Standard PiP layout for tutorials:**

| Layout | Upper clip position | Scale | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Bottom-right corner | X: 70%, Y: 65% | 30% | Most common for talking head overlays |
| Bottom-left corner | X: 2%, Y: 65% | 30% | Use when screen action is in the bottom-right |
| Top-right corner | X: 70%, Y: 2% | 30% | Use when screen action is in the lower portion |
| Split screen (50/50) | Left: X: 0%, Y: 0% — Right: X: 50%, Y: 0% | 50% each | Comparison or side-by-side tutorial steps |

**How-to:**

1. Place the main clip (screen recording) on V1.
2. Place the overlay clip (talking head) on V2 with the same time range.
3. Add a `Composite` or `Composite & Transform` composition to the V2 clip.
4. In the Effect Stack, set the geometry to your desired position and scale.
5. Keyframe the geometry to animate the PiP in/out if needed (e.g., slide in from off-screen at the start of a segment).

> **ForgeFrame:** Use `/ff-composite-pip` to place a PiP overlay automatically:
>
> ```
> /ff-composite-pip
> Input: main="screen-recording.mp4", overlay="talking-head.mp4",
>        position="bottom-right", scale=0.30, fade_in=true
> Output: timeline updated with Composite composition and keyframed geometry
> ```
>
> You can also do this manually: place the overlay clip on V2, add a Composite composition, and set the geometry rectangle to 30% scale in the Effect Stack.

---

## When NOT to Use Transitions

This is the section most tutorials skip.

**The default should always be a hard cut.** Every other transition choice is a deliberate departure from that default — and departures need justification.

### The questions to ask before adding a transition

1. **Does this serve the narrative?** A dissolve can signal the passage of time. A wipe can suggest geographic change. A slide can reinforce a "moving forward" theme. If you cannot articulate why a transition fits the story at this specific moment, use a cut.

2. **Am I hiding a problem?** Transitions are often added to disguise awkward cuts, jump cuts, or mismatched audio. Fix the underlying problem instead. A dissolve over a jump cut usually looks worse than the jump cut itself.

3. **Is this consistent with my edit so far?** One wipe in a video of hard cuts draws massive attention to itself. Either commit to a style throughout a sequence or stay with cuts.

4. **Am I compensating for missing B-roll?** If a transition feels necessary because a static talking-head cut feels boring, the answer is more B-roll, not a dissolve.

### Transitions that almost always signal a mistake

| Transition | The problem it usually signals |
|---|---|
| Slow dissolve between two similar shots | Jump cut disguise — use a cutaway instead |
| Wipe in a corporate tutorial | Draws attention to itself; rarely serves the content |
| Star wipe, clock wipe | Almost never appropriate in non-comedic content |
| Any transition applied uniformly to every cut | The editor hasn't thought about each cut individually |
| Dissolve at every scene change | Passage-of-time effect loses meaning through repetition |

### When transitions genuinely work

- **Dissolve:** Time lapse, dream sequences, memory sequences, end-of-section soft landings before a chapter card
- **Wipe:** Reveal moments where you want the viewer to notice the transition (deliberately stylized)
- **Slide:** High-energy sequences, product reveals, or branded transitions where motion graphics are part of the identity
- **Fade to black / from black:** Opening and closing segments; separating major structural sections of a long video
- **J/L cuts:** Always. Use these by default for every conversational cut.

> [!tip] Restraint is craft
> Tutorial creators who use hard cuts and J/L cuts exclusively often produce cleaner, faster-paced content than creators who reach for transitions as decoration. Train yourself to cut first, then ask if a transition adds anything.

---

## Quick Reference

### Transition Type Selector

| You want to... | Use this |
|---|---|
| Connect two clips invisibly | Hard cut + J/L cut for audio |
| Show passage of time | Short dissolve (0.5--1 sec) |
| Wipe to reveal new content | Wipe composition (bar or iris) |
| Layer a talking head over screen recording | PiP with Composite composition |
| Animate text or graphics entering the frame | Slide composition or Transform + keyframes |
| Fade to black between sections | Fade out / Fade in on single clips |

### Composition Type Reference

| Composition | Best for | Track setup needed |
|---|---|---|
| Mix (same-track) | Simple dissolves between cuts | Same track |
| Dissolve | Cross-fades with minimal control | Two-track overlap |
| Wipe | Directional or shaped reveals | Two-track overlap |
| Slide | Movement-based transitions | Two-track overlap |
| Composite | PiP, opacity, animated geometry | Two-track overlap |

content.text:
I've done my audit. Here's the design I'm proposing — please review before I make changes.

## Audit: Parallax Building Blocks

| Parallax step | Primitive | MCP status |
|---|---|---|
| Layer PNGs on multiple tracks | `track_add`, `clip_insert`, `media_ingest` | ✅ present |
| Chroma key green screen | chroma filter | ✅ `effect_chroma_key` / `_advanced` |
| Blur (shadows, depth-of-field) | boxblur | ✅ via `effect_add("avfilter.boxblur", …)` |
| Flip X/Y (character facing direction) | mirror | ✅ via `effect_add("mirror", …)` |
| Per-clip Transform (scale / position / rotate) | qtblend filter with `rect` | ⚠️ **works via raw `effect_add("qtblend", …)` but undiscoverable and awkward to keyframe** |
| Keyframed animation on transform | `effect_keyframe_set_rect` | ✅ present (once a qtblend filter is on the clip) |
| Shadow shapes under characters/props | title-based ellipse, or asset PNG | ❌ no shape generator — but workaroundable with a shadow PNG in the B-roll library |
| Depth-of-field blur on foreground | boxblur | ✅ |
| Color grading finish | `color_apply_lut` | ✅ |

## Recommendation

**One small MCP addition, no new skill, and a guide chapter update.**

### 1. Add `effect_transform` (one wrapper, ~60 LOC)

A thin wrapper around a `qtblend` filter with clean parameters:

```
effect_transform(
    workspace_path, project_file, track, clip,
    scale=1.0, pos_x=0, pos_y=0,
    rotation=0.0, opacity=1.0,
    flip_x=False, flip_y=False,
)
```

Under the hood: inserts a `qtblend` filter with the standard `rect` (`x y w h opacity`), `rotation`, and optionally stacks a `mirror` for flips. After that, users can keyframe the animation with the existing `effect_keyframe_set_rect(..., property="rect", keyframes=...)`.

This is the one missing primitive that gates parallax ergonomics.

### 2. Skip a dedicated skill

Parallax is a one-off stylistic effect, not a workflow. A skill would mostly just recite the chapter we're about to write. The handbook entry is the right home for it.

### 3. Update `docs/video-editing-guide/08-transitions-and-compositing.md`

Add a "Parallax Scenes (Layered 2.5D)" section at the end, with:
- **Concept:** why parallax works (closer objects move more).
- **Manual Kdenlive recipe:** mirrors the tutorial video — import PNGs, chroma key, Transform effect per layer, shadow circles, box blur on foreground, keyframed scale+position with layer-proportional amounts.
- **MCP recipe:** same steps mapped to tools — `media_ingest`, `track_add` per depth layer, `clip_insert`, `effect_chroma_key`, `effect_transform`, `effect_keyframe_set_rect`, `effect_add("avfilter.boxblur", …)`.
- **The parallax formula:** the movement/scale ratio table (e.g., furthest layer 1×, mid 2×, foreground 3×).
- **"Bring your own shadow" note:** keep a `shadow-ellipse.png` in the B-roll library.

### Out of scope

- Shape/shadow primitive generator — acceptable gap; covered by asset-library convention.
- Dedicated `parallax_animate` helper tool — could add later if this effect becomes routine; skip for now (YAGNI).

---

**Questions before I proceed:**

1. OK to add `effect_transform` as the single new MCP tool, and skip both the shape generator and a parallax-specific skill?
2. Should the guide section live in **Chapter 8 (Transitions & Compositing)** as I've proposed, or would you rather it go in **Chapter 12 (Effects, Titles & Graphics)**? Chapter 8 fits better IMO because it's fundamentally a compositing technique.

content.content:
1	"""Mask pipeline: shapes, MLT XML builders, and data models.
2	
3	Pure-logic module. No filesystem I/O, no MCP, no KdenliveProject mutation.
4	Follows the ``pipelines/stack_ops.py`` pattern: module-level regex/constants,
5	typed helpers, thorough docstrings, no side effects.
6	
7	See ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking.md`` (master spec) and
8	``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/sub-spec-1-mask-pipeline.md`` (phase spec).
9	
10	Kdenlive schema corrections (verified via ``/usr/share/kdenlive/effects/rotoscoping.xml``):
11	
12	* Rotoscoping MLT service is ``rotoscoping``. Spline property is named
13	  ``spline`` (type ``roto-spline``). Feather passes property is
14	  ``feather_passes`` (NOT ``passes``). Valid ``alpha_operation`` values are
15	  ``clear | max | min | add | sub``.
16	* Basic chroma key uses MLT service ``chroma`` with properties ``key`` and
17	  ``variance``. No ``blend`` property on this filter.
18	* Advanced chroma key uses MLT service ``avfilter.hsvkey`` with properties
19	  ``av.hue``, ``av.sat``, ``av.val``, ``av.similarity``, ``av.blend``.
20	* Object mask wraps ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot`` (stock shape-into-alpha).
21	
22	The rotoscoping filter produced here is the PLAIN form. Sub-Spec 2's
23	``apply_mask_to_effect`` converts it to ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` with
24	``filter.*`` prefixed properties when routing alpha onto another effect.
25	
26	Alpha routing (Sub-Spec 2)
27	--------------------------
28	
29	Kdenlive does NOT route mask alpha via a single property on the target
30	filter. Instead it uses a three-filter sandwich:
31	
32	* ``mask_start-<variant>`` -- snapshots the incoming frame and embeds the
33	  inner mask filter's parameters via ``filter.*`` prefixed properties.
34	* one or more intermediate filters -- the effects to be masked.
35	* ``mask_apply`` -- composites the masked result back over the snapshot
36	  using the ``qtblend`` transition.
37	
38	``apply_mask_to_effect`` performs this wrap in place: it converts the
39	plain rotoscoping / object_mask filter at ``mask_effect_index`` into the
40	corresponding ``mask_start`` form and inserts a ``mask_apply`` filter
41	immediately after ``target_effect_index``. Effects appended AFTER the
42	``mask_apply`` filter are OUTSIDE the sandwich and are NOT masked.
43	
44	See ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/sub-spec-2-alpha-routing.md`` for
45	the escalation trail that confirmed this behavior.
46	"""
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import colorsys
50	import json
51	import logging
52	import math
53	import re
54	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
55	from typing import Literal
56	
57	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
58	
59	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
60	
61	
62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Constants
64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	
66	# Normalization table for alpha_operation aliases → canonical MLT tokens.
67	ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT: dict[str, str] = {
68	    "clear": "clear",
69	    "max": "max",
70	    "min": "min",
71	    "add": "add",
72	    "sub": "sub",
73	    # Legacy / display-name aliases accepted by this builder, normalized to
74	    # the five MLT-canonical tokens above.
75	    "write_on_clear": "clear",
76	    "maximum": "max",
77	    "minimum": "min",
78	    "subtract": "sub",
79	}
80	
81	_COLOR_HEX_RE = re.compile(r"^#([0-9a-fA-F]{6}|[0-9a-fA-F]{8})$")
82	
83	
84	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
85	# Helpers
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	
88	def _check_normalized(value: float, label: str) -> None:
89	    """Raise ``ValueError`` if ``value`` is outside ``[0, 1]``."""
90	    if value < 0.0 or value > 1.0:
91	        raise ValueError(f"{label}={value} out of [0,1]")
92	
93	
94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	# Data models
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	class MaskShape(BaseModel):
99	    """Shape descriptor sampled into a normalized point sequence."""
100	
101	    kind: Literal["rect", "ellipse", "polygon"]
102	    bounds: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0)
103	    points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...] = ()
104	    sample_count: int = Field(default=32, ge=4)
105	
106	
107	class MaskParams(BaseModel):
108	    """Rotoscoping mask parameters, as consumed by ``build_rotoscoping_xml``."""
109	
110	    points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]
111	    feather: int = Field(default=0, ge=0, le=500)
112	    feather_passes: int = Field(default=1, ge=1, le=20)
113	    alpha_operation: Literal["clear", "max", "min", "add", "sub"] = "add"
114	
115	    @field_validator("points")
116	    @classmethod
117	    def _validate_points(
118	        cls, v: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]
119	    ) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]:
120	        for (x, y) in v:
121	            _check_normalized(x, "point.x")
122	            _check_normalized(y, "point.y")
123	        return v
124	
125	
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	# Shape sampling
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	def shape_to_points(shape: MaskShape) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]:
131	    """Convert a :class:`MaskShape` to a normalized point sequence.
132	
133	    * ``rect`` → 4 corners clockwise from top-left.
134	    * ``ellipse`` → ``sample_count`` points, first at 3 o'clock.
135	    * ``polygon`` → passthrough (requires at least 3 points).
136	    """
137	    x, y, w, h = shape.bounds
138	    if shape.kind == "rect":
139	        corners = ((x, y), (x + w, y), (x + w, y + h), (x, y + h))
140	        for (cx, cy) in corners:
141	            _check_normalized(cx, "bounds.x")
142	            _check_normalized(cy, "bounds.y")
143	        return corners
144	
145	    if shape.kind == "ellipse":
146	        n = shape.sample_count
147	        if n < 4:
148	            raise ValueError(
149	                f"ellipse sample_count={n} must be >= 4"
150	            )
151	        cx = x + w / 2.0
152	        cy = y + h / 2.0
153	        rx = w / 2.0
154	        ry = h / 2.0
155	        pts: list[tuple[float, float]] = []
156	        for i in range(n):
157	            theta = 2.0 * math.pi * i / n
158	            px = cx + rx * math.cos(theta)
159	            py = cy + ry * math.sin(theta)
160	            _check_normalized(px, "ellipse.x")
161	            _check_normalized(py, "ellipse.y")
162	            pts.append((px, py))
163	        return tuple(pts)
164	
165	    # polygon
166	    if len(shape.points) < 3:
167	        raise ValueError("polygon requires at least 3 points")
168	    for (px, py) in shape.points:
169	        _check_normalized(px, "polygon.x")
170	        _check_normalized(py, "polygon.y")
171	    return tuple(shape.points)
172	
173	
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# Color
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	def color_to_mlt_hex(value: str | int) -> str:
179	    """Convert a color to MLT's ``0xRRGGBBAA`` string form.
180	
181	    Accepts ``#RRGGBB`` (alpha defaulted to ``ff``), ``#RRGGBBAA``, or an
182	    integer that is treated as a 32-bit RGBA value.
183	    """
184	    if isinstance(value, bool):  # bool is subclass of int -- reject it explicitly
185	        raise ValueError(
186	            f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
187	        )
188	    if isinstance(value, int):
189	        if value < 0 or value > 0xFFFFFFFF:
190	            raise ValueError(
191	                f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected 32-bit int"
192	            )
193	        return f"0x{value:08x}"
194	    if isinstance(value, str):
195	        m = _COLOR_HEX_RE.match(value)
196	        if not m:
197	            raise ValueError(
198	                f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
199	            )
200	        hexpart = m.group(1).lower()
201	        if len(hexpart) == 6:
202	            hexpart += "ff"
203	        return f"0x{hexpart}"
204	    raise ValueError(
205	        f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
206	    )
207	
208	
209	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
210	# XML building helpers
211	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	
213	def _make_filter(
214	    mlt_service: str,
215	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
216	    props: list[tuple[str, str]],
217	) -> str:
218	    track, clip = clip_ref
219	    root = ET.Element(
220	        "filter",
221	        {
222	            "mlt_service": mlt_service,
223	            "track": str(track),
224	            "clip_index": str(clip),
225	        },
226	    )
227	    for name, text in props:
228	        el = ET.SubElement(root, "property", {"name": name})
229	        el.text = text
230	    return ET.tostring(root, encoding="unicode")
231	
232	
233	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	# Rotoscoping
235	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
236	
237	def _spline_json(points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]) -> str:
238	    """Serialize a point list to Kdenlive's ``roto-spline`` format.
239	
240	    v1 emits a single keyframe at frame 0 with linear (handle == point)
241	    connections: ``{"0": [[[x,y],[x,y],[x,y]], ...]}``.
242	    """
243	    frame0 = [[[x, y], [x, y], [x, y]] for (x, y) in points]
244	    return json.dumps({"0": frame0})
245	
246	
247	def build_rotoscoping_xml(
248	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], mask: MaskParams
249	) -> str:
250	    """Emit the plain ``rotoscoping`` filter XML for a clip.
251	
252	    Sub-Spec 2's alpha-routing pass will later wrap this into the
253	    ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` form with ``filter.*`` prefixed properties.
254	    """
255	    normalized = ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT.get(mask.alpha_operation)
256	    if normalized is None:
257	        raise ValueError(
258	            f"unknown alpha_operation: {mask.alpha_operation!r}"
259	        )
260	    spline = _spline_json(mask.points)
261	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
262	        ("mlt_service", "rotoscoping"),
263	        ("kdenlive_id", "rotoscoping"),
264	        ("mode", "alpha"),
265	        ("alpha_operation", normalized),
266	        ("invert", "0"),
267	        ("feather", str(mask.feather)),
268	        ("feather_passes", str(mask.feather_passes)),
269	        ("spline", spline),
270	    ]
271	    return _make_filter("rotoscoping", clip_ref, props)
272	
273	
274	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
275	# Object mask (frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot surrogate)
276	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
277	
278	def build_object_mask_xml(
279	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], params: dict
280	) -> str:
281	    """Build an ``object_mask`` filter by wrapping ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot``.
282	
283	    Kdenlive ships no AI object-detector; this builder emulates
284	    ``object_mask`` via the stock alpha-spot shape filter. See
285	    ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/index.md`` -- Object mask section.
286	    """
287	    shape = int(params.get("shape", 0))
288	    pos_x = float(params.get("position_x", 0.5))
289	    pos_y = float(params.get("position_y", 0.5))
290	    size_x = float(params.get("size_x", 0.5))
291	    size_y = float(params.get("size_y", 0.5))
292	    tilt = float(params.get("tilt", 0.5))
293	    alpha_op = int(params.get("alpha_operation", 0))
294	    threshold = float(params.get("threshold", 0.5))
295	    enabled = bool(params.get("enabled", True))
296	
297	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
298	        ("mlt_service", "frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot"),
299	        ("kdenlive_id", "frei0r_alpha0ps_alphaspot"),
300	        ("0", str(shape)),
301	        ("1", str(pos_x)),
302	        ("2", str(pos_y)),
303	        ("3", str(size_x)),
304	        ("4", str(size_y)),
305	        ("5", str(tilt)),
306	        ("6", str(alpha_op)),
307	        ("7", str(threshold)),
308	    ]
309	    if not enabled:
310	        props.append(("disable", "1"))
311	    return _make_filter("frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot", clip_ref, props)
312	
313	
314	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
315	# Chroma key -- basic
316	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
317	
318	def build_chroma_key_xml(
319	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
320	    color: str,
321	    tolerance: float,
322	    blend: float = 0.0,
323	) -> str:
324	    """Build a basic ``chroma`` MLT filter.
325	
326	    The ``blend`` argument is accepted for API symmetry but ignored; the
327	    basic chroma filter has no blend property. A warning is logged if a
328	    non-zero value is supplied.
329	    """
330	    if blend:
331	        logger.warning(
332	            "build_chroma_key_xml: blend=%s ignored (basic chroma has no blend property)",
333	            blend,
334	        )
335	    key_hex = color_to_mlt_hex(color)
336	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
337	        ("mlt_service", "chroma"),
338	        ("kdenlive_id", "chroma"),
339	        ("key", key_hex),
340	        ("variance", f"{tolerance}"),
341	    ]
342	    return _make_filter("chroma", clip_ref, props)
343	
344	
345	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
346	# Chroma key -- advanced (avfilter.hsvkey)
347	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
348	
349	def _hex_to_hsv(color: str) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
350	    """Convert ``#RRGGBB[AA]`` to HSV with H in degrees (0-360)."""
351	    m = _COLOR_HEX_RE.match(color)
352	    if not m:
353	        raise ValueError(
354	            f"invalid color: {color!r} -- expected #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA"
355	        )
356	    hexpart = m.group(1)
357	    r = int(hexpart[0:2], 16) / 255.0
358	    g = int(hexpart[2:4], 16) / 255.0
359	    b = int(hexpart[4:6], 16) / 255.0
360	    h, s, v = colorsys.rgb_to_hsv(r, g, b)
361	    return (h * 360.0, s, v)
362	
363	
364	def build_chroma_key_advanced_xml(
365	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
366	    color: str,
367	    tolerance_near: float,
368	    tolerance_far: float,
369	    edge_smooth: float = 0.0,
370	    spill_suppression: float = 0.0,
371	) -> str:
372	    """Build an ``avfilter.hsvkey`` advanced chroma filter."""
373	    if tolerance_far < tolerance_near:
374	        raise ValueError("tolerance_far must be >= tolerance_near")
375	    hue, sat, val = _hex_to_hsv(color)
376	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
377	        ("mlt_service", "avfilter.hsvkey"),
378	        ("kdenlive_id", "avfilter_hsvkey"),
379	        ("av.hue", f"{hue}"),
380	        ("av.sat", f"{sat}"),
381	        ("av.val", f"{val}"),
382	        ("av.similarity", f"{tolerance_near}"),
383	        ("av.blend", f"{edge_smooth}"),
384	    ]
385	    if spill_suppression > 0:
386	        props.append(("av.spill_suppression", f"{spill_suppression}"))
387	    return _make_filter("avfilter.hsvkey", clip_ref, props)
388	
389	
390	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
391	# Alpha routing (Sub-Spec 2): mask_start / mask_apply sandwich
392	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
393	
394	MASK_START_SERVICES: tuple[str, ...] = ("mask_start",)
395	MASK_APPLY_SERVICE: str = "mask_apply"
396	MASK_CAPABLE_INNER_SERVICES: tuple[str, ...] = (
397	    "rotoscoping",
398	    "frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot",
399	    "shape",
400	)
401	
402	# Property names on the plain rotoscoping filter that should be preserved
403	# (with a ``filter.`` prefix) when converting to the ``mask_start`` sandwich.
404	_ROTOSCOPING_INNER_PROPS: tuple[str, ...] = (
405	    "mode",
406	    "alpha_operation",
407	    "invert",
408	    "feather",
409	    "feather_passes",
410	    "spline",
411	)
412	
413	# Property names on the object_mask (frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot) filter that
414	# should be preserved (with a ``filter.`` prefix) when converting.
415	_OBJECT_MASK_INNER_PROPS: tuple[str, ...] = (
416	    "0", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7",
417	)
418	
419	
420	def build_mask_start_rotoscoping_xml(
421	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], mask: MaskParams
422	) -> str:
423	    """Emit a ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` filter (sandwich opener).
424	
425	    Wraps a rotoscoping mask's parameters via ``filter.*`` prefixed
426	    properties, matching ``/usr/share/kdenlive/effects/mask_start_rotoscoping.xml``.
427	    """
428	    normalized = ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT.get(mask.alpha_operation)
429	    if normalized is None:
430	        raise ValueError(
431	            f"unknown alpha_operation: {mask.alpha_operation!r}"
432	        )
433	    spline = _spline_json(mask.points)
434	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
435	        ("mlt_service", "mask_start"),
436	        ("kdenlive_id", "mask_start-rotoscoping"),
437	        ("filter", "rotoscoping"),
438	        ("filter.mode", "alpha"),
439	        ("filter.alpha_operation", normalized),
440	        ("filter.invert", "0"),
441	        ("filter.feather", str(mask.feather)),
442	        ("filter.feather_passes", str(mask.feather_passes)),
443	        ("filter.spline", spline),
444	    ]
445	    return _make_filter("mask_start", clip_ref, props)
446	
447	
448	def build_mask_apply_xml(clip_ref: tuple[int, int]) -> str:
449	    """Emit a ``mask_apply`` filter (sandwich closer).
450	
451	    Composites the masked result back via a ``qtblend`` transition.
452	    """
453	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
454	        ("mlt_service", "mask_apply"),
455	        ("kdenlive_id", "mask_apply"),
456	        ("transition", "qtblend"),
457	    ]
458	    return _make_filter("mask_apply", clip_ref, props)
459	
460	
461	def _build_mask_start_from_existing(
462	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
463	    inner_service: str,
464	    existing_props: dict[str, str],
465	) -> str:
466	    """Build a ``mask_start`` filter by promoting an existing plain filter's
467	    properties to ``filter.*`` prefixed form.
468	
469	    ``inner_service`` is the MLT service of the plain inner filter
470	    (``rotoscoping`` or ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot``).
471	    """
472	    if inner_service == "rotoscoping":
473	        inner_keys = _ROTOSCOPING_INNER_PROPS
474	        kdenlive_id = "mask_start-rotoscoping"
475	        filter_value = "rotoscoping"
476	    elif inner_service in ("frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot", "shape"):
477	        inner_keys = _OBJECT_MASK_INNER_PROPS
478	        kdenlive_id = "mask_start-shape"
479	        filter_value = inner_service
480	    else:
481	        raise ValueError(
482	            f"cannot convert service {inner_service!r} to mask_start form"
483	        )
484	
485	    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
486	        ("mlt_service", "mask_start"),
487	        ("kdenlive_id", kdenlive_id),
488	        ("filter", filter_value),
489	    ]
490	    for key in inner_keys:
491	        if key in existing_props:
492	            props.append((f"filter.{key}", existing_props[key]))
493	    return _make_filter("mask_start", clip_ref, props)
494	
495	
496	def _rewrite_mask_to_mask_start(
497	    project,
498	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
499	    mask_effect_index: int,
500	    mask_service: str,
501	) -> None:
502	    """In-place conversion: replace the plain mask filter at
503	    ``mask_effect_index`` with its ``mask_start`` sandwich-opener form,
504	    promoting its properties to ``filter.*`` prefixed names.
505	    """
506	    # Imported here to avoid a module-load cycle with the patcher module.
507	    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher
508	
509	    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
510	    existing_props = dict(filters[mask_effect_index]["properties"])
511	    new_xml = _build_mask_start_from_existing(
512	        clip_ref, mask_service, existing_props
513	    )
514	    patcher.remove_effect(project, clip_ref, mask_effect_index)
515	    patcher.insert_effect_xml(project, clip_ref, new_xml, mask_effect_index)
516	
517	
518	def _insert_mask_apply(
519	    project,
520	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
521	    position: int,
522	) -> None:
523	    """Insert a ``mask_apply`` filter at ``position`` in the clip's stack."""
524	    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher
525	    patcher.insert_effect_xml(
526	        project, clip_ref, build_mask_apply_xml(clip_ref), position
527	    )
528	
529	
530	def apply_mask_to_effect(
531	    project,
532	    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
533	    mask_effect_index: int,
534	    target_effect_index: int,
535	) -> dict:
536	    """Wrap a target filter with the mask_start / mask_apply sandwich.
537	
538	    Converts the plain rotoscoping / object_mask filter at
539	    ``mask_effect_index`` into its ``mask_start`` form (if not already),
540	    ensures the mask precedes the target in the stack (reordering if
541	    needed), and inserts a ``mask_apply`` filter immediately after the
542	    target (unless one already exists downstream).
543	
544	    Returns a dict:
545	
546	    * ``reordered`` -- True if the stack was reordered.
547	    * ``mask_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the mask filter.
548	    * ``target_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the target filter.
549	    * ``mask_apply_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the mask_apply
550	      filter.
551	    * ``converted_to_sandwich`` -- True if this call converted a plain
552	      mask filter to its ``mask_start`` form (i.e., first wrap).
553	
554	    Raises ``IndexError`` if either index is out of range. Raises
555	    ``ValueError`` if the filter at ``mask_effect_index`` is not a
556	    recognized mask filter.
557	    """
558	    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher
559	
560	    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
561	    n = len(filters)
562	    if mask_effect_index < 0 or mask_effect_index >= n:
563	        raise IndexError(
564	            f"mask_effect_index {mask_effect_index} out of range "
565	            f"(clip has {n} filters)"
566	        )
567	    if target_effect_index < 0 or target_effect_index >= n:
568	        raise IndexError(
569	            f"target_effect_index {target_effect_index} out of range "
570	            f"(clip has {n} filters)"
571	        )
572	
573	    mask_filter = filters[mask_effect_index]
574	    mask_service = mask_filter["mlt_service"]
575	
576	    valid_services = ("mask_start", *MASK_CAPABLE_INNER_SERVICES)
577	    if mask_service not in valid_services:
578	        raise ValueError(
579	            f"effect at index {mask_effect_index} has service "
580	            f"{mask_service!r}; expected one of {valid_services}"
581	        )
582	
583	    # Step 1: conversion. Plain filter -> mask_start form.
584	    converted = False
585	    if mask_service != "mask_start":
586	        _rewrite_mask_to_mask_start(
587	            project, clip_ref, mask_effect_index, mask_service
588	        )
589	        converted = True
590	
591	    # Step 2: reorder if mask index is not above target index.
592	    reordered = False
593	    # Refresh after potential conversion (indices unchanged, but safe).
594	    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
595	    if mask_effect_index >= target_effect_index:
596	        patcher.reorder_effects(
597	            project, clip_ref,
598	            from_index=mask_effect_index,
599	            to_index=target_effect_index,
600	        )
601	        reordered = True
602	        # After moving mask to target's slot, target shifts up by one
603	        # (mask was below or at target).
604	        new_mask_index = target_effect_index
605	        new_target_index = target_effect_index + 1
606	    else:
607	        new_mask_index = mask_effect_index
608	        new_target_index = target_effect_index
609	
610	    # Step 3: ensure a mask_apply exists downstream of the target.
611	    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
612	    mask_apply_index: int | None = None
613	    for i, f in enumerate(filters):
614	        if i > new_target_index and f["mlt_service"] == MASK_APPLY_SERVICE:
615	            mask_apply_index = i
616	            break
617	
618	    if mask_apply_index is None:
619	        _insert_mask_apply(project, clip_ref, new_target_index + 1)
620	        mask_apply_index = new_target_index + 1
621	
622	    return {
623	        "reordered": reordered,
624	        "mask_effect_index": new_mask_index,
625	        "target_effect_index": new_target_index,
626	        "mask_apply_effect_index": mask_apply_index,
627	        "converted_to_sandwich": converted,
628	    }
629

file.content:
"""Mask pipeline: shapes, MLT XML builders, and data models.

Pure-logic module. No filesystem I/O, no MCP, no KdenliveProject mutation.
Follows the ``pipelines/stack_ops.py`` pattern: module-level regex/constants,
typed helpers, thorough docstrings, no side effects.

See ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking.md`` (master spec) and
``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/sub-spec-1-mask-pipeline.md`` (phase spec).

Kdenlive schema corrections (verified via ``/usr/share/kdenlive/effects/rotoscoping.xml``):

* Rotoscoping MLT service is ``rotoscoping``. Spline property is named
  ``spline`` (type ``roto-spline``). Feather passes property is
  ``feather_passes`` (NOT ``passes``). Valid ``alpha_operation`` values are
  ``clear | max | min | add | sub``.
* Basic chroma key uses MLT service ``chroma`` with properties ``key`` and
  ``variance``. No ``blend`` property on this filter.
* Advanced chroma key uses MLT service ``avfilter.hsvkey`` with properties
  ``av.hue``, ``av.sat``, ``av.val``, ``av.similarity``, ``av.blend``.
* Object mask wraps ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot`` (stock shape-into-alpha).

The rotoscoping filter produced here is the PLAIN form. Sub-Spec 2's
``apply_mask_to_effect`` converts it to ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` with
``filter.*`` prefixed properties when routing alpha onto another effect.

Alpha routing (Sub-Spec 2)
--------------------------

Kdenlive does NOT route mask alpha via a single property on the target
filter. Instead it uses a three-filter sandwich:

* ``mask_start-<variant>`` -- snapshots the incoming frame and embeds the
  inner mask filter's parameters via ``filter.*`` prefixed properties.
* one or more intermediate filters -- the effects to be masked.
* ``mask_apply`` -- composites the masked result back over the snapshot
  using the ``qtblend`` transition.

``apply_mask_to_effect`` performs this wrap in place: it converts the
plain rotoscoping / object_mask filter at ``mask_effect_index`` into the
corresponding ``mask_start`` form and inserts a ``mask_apply`` filter
immediately after ``target_effect_index``. Effects appended AFTER the
``mask_apply`` filter are OUTSIDE the sandwich and are NOT masked.

See ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/sub-spec-2-alpha-routing.md`` for
the escalation trail that confirmed this behavior.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import colorsys
import json
import logging
import math
import re
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from typing import Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Normalization table for alpha_operation aliases → canonical MLT tokens.
ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT: dict[str, str] = {
    "clear": "clear",
    "max": "max",
    "min": "min",
    "add": "add",
    "sub": "sub",
    # Legacy / display-name aliases accepted by this builder, normalized to
    # the five MLT-canonical tokens above.
    "write_on_clear": "clear",
    "maximum": "max",
    "minimum": "min",
    "subtract": "sub",
}

_COLOR_HEX_RE = re.compile(r"^#([0-9a-fA-F]{6}|[0-9a-fA-F]{8})$")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _check_normalized(value: float, label: str) -> None:
    """Raise ``ValueError`` if ``value`` is outside ``[0, 1]``."""
    if value < 0.0 or value > 1.0:
        raise ValueError(f"{label}={value} out of [0,1]")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class MaskShape(BaseModel):
    """Shape descriptor sampled into a normalized point sequence."""

    kind: Literal["rect", "ellipse", "polygon"]
    bounds: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0)
    points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...] = ()
    sample_count: int = Field(default=32, ge=4)


class MaskParams(BaseModel):
    """Rotoscoping mask parameters, as consumed by ``build_rotoscoping_xml``."""

    points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]
    feather: int = Field(default=0, ge=0, le=500)
    feather_passes: int = Field(default=1, ge=1, le=20)
    alpha_operation: Literal["clear", "max", "min", "add", "sub"] = "add"

    @field_validator("points")
    @classmethod
    def _validate_points(
        cls, v: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]
    ) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]:
        for (x, y) in v:
            _check_normalized(x, "point.x")
            _check_normalized(y, "point.y")
        return v


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shape sampling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def shape_to_points(shape: MaskShape) -> tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]:
    """Convert a :class:`MaskShape` to a normalized point sequence.

    * ``rect`` → 4 corners clockwise from top-left.
    * ``ellipse`` → ``sample_count`` points, first at 3 o'clock.
    * ``polygon`` → passthrough (requires at least 3 points).
    """
    x, y, w, h = shape.bounds
    if shape.kind == "rect":
        corners = ((x, y), (x + w, y), (x + w, y + h), (x, y + h))
        for (cx, cy) in corners:
            _check_normalized(cx, "bounds.x")
            _check_normalized(cy, "bounds.y")
        return corners

    if shape.kind == "ellipse":
        n = shape.sample_count
        if n < 4:
            raise ValueError(
                f"ellipse sample_count={n} must be >= 4"
            )
        cx = x + w / 2.0
        cy = y + h / 2.0
        rx = w / 2.0
        ry = h / 2.0
        pts: list[tuple[float, float]] = []
        for i in range(n):
            theta = 2.0 * math.pi * i / n
            px = cx + rx * math.cos(theta)
            py = cy + ry * math.sin(theta)
            _check_normalized(px, "ellipse.x")
            _check_normalized(py, "ellipse.y")
            pts.append((px, py))
        return tuple(pts)

    # polygon
    if len(shape.points) < 3:
        raise ValueError("polygon requires at least 3 points")
    for (px, py) in shape.points:
        _check_normalized(px, "polygon.x")
        _check_normalized(py, "polygon.y")
    return tuple(shape.points)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Color
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def color_to_mlt_hex(value: str | int) -> str:
    """Convert a color to MLT's ``0xRRGGBBAA`` string form.

    Accepts ``#RRGGBB`` (alpha defaulted to ``ff``), ``#RRGGBBAA``, or an
    integer that is treated as a 32-bit RGBA value.
    """
    if isinstance(value, bool):  # bool is subclass of int -- reject it explicitly
        raise ValueError(
            f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
        )
    if isinstance(value, int):
        if value < 0 or value > 0xFFFFFFFF:
            raise ValueError(
                f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected 32-bit int"
            )
        return f"0x{value:08x}"
    if isinstance(value, str):
        m = _COLOR_HEX_RE.match(value)
        if not m:
            raise ValueError(
                f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
            )
        hexpart = m.group(1).lower()
        if len(hexpart) == 6:
            hexpart += "ff"
        return f"0x{hexpart}"
    raise ValueError(
        f"invalid color: {value!r} -- expected #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA, or int"
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# XML building helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_filter(
    mlt_service: str,
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    props: list[tuple[str, str]],
) -> str:
    track, clip = clip_ref
    root = ET.Element(
        "filter",
        {
            "mlt_service": mlt_service,
            "track": str(track),
            "clip_index": str(clip),
        },
    )
    for name, text in props:
        el = ET.SubElement(root, "property", {"name": name})
        el.text = text
    return ET.tostring(root, encoding="unicode")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rotoscoping
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _spline_json(points: tuple[tuple[float, float], ...]) -> str:
    """Serialize a point list to Kdenlive's ``roto-spline`` format.

    v1 emits a single keyframe at frame 0 with linear (handle == point)
    connections: ``{"0": [[[x,y],[x,y],[x,y]], ...]}``.
    """
    frame0 = [[[x, y], [x, y], [x, y]] for (x, y) in points]
    return json.dumps({"0": frame0})


def build_rotoscoping_xml(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], mask: MaskParams
) -> str:
    """Emit the plain ``rotoscoping`` filter XML for a clip.

    Sub-Spec 2's alpha-routing pass will later wrap this into the
    ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` form with ``filter.*`` prefixed properties.
    """
    normalized = ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT.get(mask.alpha_operation)
    if normalized is None:
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown alpha_operation: {mask.alpha_operation!r}"
        )
    spline = _spline_json(mask.points)
    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "rotoscoping"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "rotoscoping"),
        ("mode", "alpha"),
        ("alpha_operation", normalized),
        ("invert", "0"),
        ("feather", str(mask.feather)),
        ("feather_passes", str(mask.feather_passes)),
        ("spline", spline),
    ]
    return _make_filter("rotoscoping", clip_ref, props)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Object mask (frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot surrogate)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_object_mask_xml(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], params: dict
) -> str:
    """Build an ``object_mask`` filter by wrapping ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot``.

    Kdenlive ships no AI object-detector; this builder emulates
    ``object_mask`` via the stock alpha-spot shape filter. See
    ``docs/specs/2026-04-13-masking/index.md`` -- Object mask section.
    """
    shape = int(params.get("shape", 0))
    pos_x = float(params.get("position_x", 0.5))
    pos_y = float(params.get("position_y", 0.5))
    size_x = float(params.get("size_x", 0.5))
    size_y = float(params.get("size_y", 0.5))
    tilt = float(params.get("tilt", 0.5))
    alpha_op = int(params.get("alpha_operation", 0))
    threshold = float(params.get("threshold", 0.5))
    enabled = bool(params.get("enabled", True))

    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "frei0r_alpha0ps_alphaspot"),
        ("0", str(shape)),
        ("1", str(pos_x)),
        ("2", str(pos_y)),
        ("3", str(size_x)),
        ("4", str(size_y)),
        ("5", str(tilt)),
        ("6", str(alpha_op)),
        ("7", str(threshold)),
    ]
    if not enabled:
        props.append(("disable", "1"))
    return _make_filter("frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot", clip_ref, props)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Chroma key -- basic
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_chroma_key_xml(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    color: str,
    tolerance: float,
    blend: float = 0.0,
) -> str:
    """Build a basic ``chroma`` MLT filter.

    The ``blend`` argument is accepted for API symmetry but ignored; the
    basic chroma filter has no blend property. A warning is logged if a
    non-zero value is supplied.
    """
    if blend:
        logger.warning(
            "build_chroma_key_xml: blend=%s ignored (basic chroma has no blend property)",
            blend,
        )
    key_hex = color_to_mlt_hex(color)
    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "chroma"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "chroma"),
        ("key", key_hex),
        ("variance", f"{tolerance}"),
    ]
    return _make_filter("chroma", clip_ref, props)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Chroma key -- advanced (avfilter.hsvkey)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _hex_to_hsv(color: str) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    """Convert ``#RRGGBB[AA]`` to HSV with H in degrees (0-360)."""
    m = _COLOR_HEX_RE.match(color)
    if not m:
        raise ValueError(
            f"invalid color: {color!r} -- expected #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA"
        )
    hexpart = m.group(1)
    r = int(hexpart[0:2], 16) / 255.0
    g = int(hexpart[2:4], 16) / 255.0
    b = int(hexpart[4:6], 16) / 255.0
    h, s, v = colorsys.rgb_to_hsv(r, g, b)
    return (h * 360.0, s, v)


def build_chroma_key_advanced_xml(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    color: str,
    tolerance_near: float,
    tolerance_far: float,
    edge_smooth: float = 0.0,
    spill_suppression: float = 0.0,
) -> str:
    """Build an ``avfilter.hsvkey`` advanced chroma filter."""
    if tolerance_far < tolerance_near:
        raise ValueError("tolerance_far must be >= tolerance_near")
    hue, sat, val = _hex_to_hsv(color)
    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "avfilter.hsvkey"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "avfilter_hsvkey"),
        ("av.hue", f"{hue}"),
        ("av.sat", f"{sat}"),
        ("av.val", f"{val}"),
        ("av.similarity", f"{tolerance_near}"),
        ("av.blend", f"{edge_smooth}"),
    ]
    if spill_suppression > 0:
        props.append(("av.spill_suppression", f"{spill_suppression}"))
    return _make_filter("avfilter.hsvkey", clip_ref, props)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Alpha routing (Sub-Spec 2): mask_start / mask_apply sandwich
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MASK_START_SERVICES: tuple[str, ...] = ("mask_start",)
MASK_APPLY_SERVICE: str = "mask_apply"
MASK_CAPABLE_INNER_SERVICES: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "rotoscoping",
    "frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot",
    "shape",
)

# Property names on the plain rotoscoping filter that should be preserved
# (with a ``filter.`` prefix) when converting to the ``mask_start`` sandwich.
_ROTOSCOPING_INNER_PROPS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "mode",
    "alpha_operation",
    "invert",
    "feather",
    "feather_passes",
    "spline",
)

# Property names on the object_mask (frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot) filter that
# should be preserved (with a ``filter.`` prefix) when converting.
_OBJECT_MASK_INNER_PROPS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "0", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7",
)


def build_mask_start_rotoscoping_xml(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int], mask: MaskParams
) -> str:
    """Emit a ``mask_start-rotoscoping`` filter (sandwich opener).

    Wraps a rotoscoping mask's parameters via ``filter.*`` prefixed
    properties, matching ``/usr/share/kdenlive/effects/mask_start_rotoscoping.xml``.
    """
    normalized = ALPHA_OPERATION_TO_MLT.get(mask.alpha_operation)
    if normalized is None:
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown alpha_operation: {mask.alpha_operation!r}"
        )
    spline = _spline_json(mask.points)
    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "mask_start"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "mask_start-rotoscoping"),
        ("filter", "rotoscoping"),
        ("filter.mode", "alpha"),
        ("filter.alpha_operation", normalized),
        ("filter.invert", "0"),
        ("filter.feather", str(mask.feather)),
        ("filter.feather_passes", str(mask.feather_passes)),
        ("filter.spline", spline),
    ]
    return _make_filter("mask_start", clip_ref, props)


def build_mask_apply_xml(clip_ref: tuple[int, int]) -> str:
    """Emit a ``mask_apply`` filter (sandwich closer).

    Composites the masked result back via a ``qtblend`` transition.
    """
    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "mask_apply"),
        ("kdenlive_id", "mask_apply"),
        ("transition", "qtblend"),
    ]
    return _make_filter("mask_apply", clip_ref, props)


def _build_mask_start_from_existing(
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    inner_service: str,
    existing_props: dict[str, str],
) -> str:
    """Build a ``mask_start`` filter by promoting an existing plain filter's
    properties to ``filter.*`` prefixed form.

    ``inner_service`` is the MLT service of the plain inner filter
    (``rotoscoping`` or ``frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot``).
    """
    if inner_service == "rotoscoping":
        inner_keys = _ROTOSCOPING_INNER_PROPS
        kdenlive_id = "mask_start-rotoscoping"
        filter_value = "rotoscoping"
    elif inner_service in ("frei0r.alpha0ps_alphaspot", "shape"):
        inner_keys = _OBJECT_MASK_INNER_PROPS
        kdenlive_id = "mask_start-shape"
        filter_value = inner_service
    else:
        raise ValueError(
            f"cannot convert service {inner_service!r} to mask_start form"
        )

    props: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
        ("mlt_service", "mask_start"),
        ("kdenlive_id", kdenlive_id),
        ("filter", filter_value),
    ]
    for key in inner_keys:
        if key in existing_props:
            props.append((f"filter.{key}", existing_props[key]))
    return _make_filter("mask_start", clip_ref, props)


def _rewrite_mask_to_mask_start(
    project,
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    mask_effect_index: int,
    mask_service: str,
) -> None:
    """In-place conversion: replace the plain mask filter at
    ``mask_effect_index`` with its ``mask_start`` sandwich-opener form,
    promoting its properties to ``filter.*`` prefixed names.
    """
    # Imported here to avoid a module-load cycle with the patcher module.
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher

    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
    existing_props = dict(filters[mask_effect_index]["properties"])
    new_xml = _build_mask_start_from_existing(
        clip_ref, mask_service, existing_props
    )
    patcher.remove_effect(project, clip_ref, mask_effect_index)
    patcher.insert_effect_xml(project, clip_ref, new_xml, mask_effect_index)


def _insert_mask_apply(
    project,
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    position: int,
) -> None:
    """Insert a ``mask_apply`` filter at ``position`` in the clip's stack."""
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher
    patcher.insert_effect_xml(
        project, clip_ref, build_mask_apply_xml(clip_ref), position
    )


def apply_mask_to_effect(
    project,
    clip_ref: tuple[int, int],
    mask_effect_index: int,
    target_effect_index: int,
) -> dict:
    """Wrap a target filter with the mask_start / mask_apply sandwich.

    Converts the plain rotoscoping / object_mask filter at
    ``mask_effect_index`` into its ``mask_start`` form (if not already),
    ensures the mask precedes the target in the stack (reordering if
    needed), and inserts a ``mask_apply`` filter immediately after the
    target (unless one already exists downstream).

    Returns a dict:

    * ``reordered`` -- True if the stack was reordered.
    * ``mask_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the mask filter.
    * ``target_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the target filter.
    * ``mask_apply_effect_index`` -- final stack index of the mask_apply
      filter.
    * ``converted_to_sandwich`` -- True if this call converted a plain
      mask filter to its ``mask_start`` form (i.e., first wrap).

    Raises ``IndexError`` if either index is out of range. Raises
    ``ValueError`` if the filter at ``mask_effect_index`` is not a
    recognized mask filter.
    """
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher

    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
    n = len(filters)
    if mask_effect_index < 0 or mask_effect_index >= n:
        raise IndexError(
            f"mask_effect_index {mask_effect_index} out of range "
            f"(clip has {n} filters)"
        )
    if target_effect_index < 0 or target_effect_index >= n:
        raise IndexError(
            f"target_effect_index {target_effect_index} out of range "
            f"(clip has {n} filters)"
        )

    mask_filter = filters[mask_effect_index]
    mask_service = mask_filter["mlt_service"]

    valid_services = ("mask_start", *MASK_CAPABLE_INNER_SERVICES)
    if mask_service not in valid_services:
        raise ValueError(
            f"effect at index {mask_effect_index} has service "
            f"{mask_service!r}; expected one of {valid_services}"
        )

    # Step 1: conversion. Plain filter -> mask_start form.
    converted = False
    if mask_service != "mask_start":
        _rewrite_mask_to_mask_start(
            project, clip_ref, mask_effect_index, mask_service
        )
        converted = True

    # Step 2: reorder if mask index is not above target index.
    reordered = False
    # Refresh after potential conversion (indices unchanged, but safe).
    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
    if mask_effect_index >= target_effect_index:
        patcher.reorder_effects(
            project, clip_ref,
            from_index=mask_effect_index,
            to_index=target_effect_index,
        )
        reordered = True
        # After moving mask to target's slot, target shifts up by one
        # (mask was below or at target).
        new_mask_index = target_effect_index
        new_target_index = target_effect_index + 1
    else:
        new_mask_index = mask_effect_index
        new_target_index = target_effect_index

    # Step 3: ensure a mask_apply exists downstream of the target.
    filters = patcher.list_effects(project, clip_ref)
    mask_apply_index: int | None = None
    for i, f in enumerate(filters):
        if i > new_target_index and f["mlt_service"] == MASK_APPLY_SERVICE:
            mask_apply_index = i
            break

    if mask_apply_index is None:
        _insert_mask_apply(project, clip_ref, new_target_index + 1)
        mask_apply_index = new_target_index + 1

    return {
        "reordered": reordered,
        "mask_effect_index": new_mask_index,
        "target_effect_index": new_target_index,
        "mask_apply_effect_index": mask_apply_index,
        "converted_to_sandwich": converted,
    }

"""Integration tests for effect preset MCP tools (Sub-Spec 2).

Covers SR-13..SR-23 of the Effect Wrappers test plan.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

fastmcp = pytest.importorskip("fastmcp", reason="fastmcp not installed")

from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.server import tools
from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.server.tools import (
    effect_fade,
    effect_glitch_stack,
    flash_cut_montage,
    workspace_create,
)

FIXTURE = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures" / "keyframe_project.kdenlive"
TRACK = 2
CLIP = 0


def _make_ws(tmp_path: Path, project_name: str = "test.kdenlive") -> tuple[Path, str]:
    media_root = tmp_path / "media"
    media_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    res = workspace_create(title="Presets Test", media_root=str(media_root))
    assert res["status"] == "success", res
    ws_root = Path(res["data"]["workspace_root"])
    dest = ws_root / project_name
    shutil.copy(FIXTURE, dest)
    return ws_root, project_name


def _reparse(ws: Path, pf: str):
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive.parser import parse_project
    return parse_project(ws / pf)


def _list_filters(ws: Path, pf: str, track: int, clip: int):
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.adapters.kdenlive import patcher
    return patcher.list_effects(_reparse(ws, pf), (track, clip))


def _count_snapshots(ws: Path) -> int:
    d = ws / "projects" / "snapshots"
    if not d.exists():
        return 0
    return sum(1 for p in d.iterdir() if p.is_dir())


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _isolate_vault(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(tools, "_resolve_vault_root_for_tools", lambda: None)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SR-13: effect_glitch_stack inserts 5 filters in order
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_sr13_glitch_stack_inserts_five_filters_in_order(tmp_path):
    ws, pf = _make_ws(tmp_path)
    before = _count_snapshots(ws)
    out = effect_glitch_stack(
        workspace_path=str(ws), project_file=pf,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP, intensity=0.5,
    )
    assert out["status"] == "success", out
    assert out["data"]["filter_count"] == 5
    assert out["data"]["first_effect_index"] >= 0
    assert "snapshot_id" in out["data"]

    filters = _list_filters(ws, pf, TRACK, CLIP)
    services = [f["mlt_service"] for f in filters]
    # Fixture ships with an existing affine filter; the 5 glitch filters are
    # appended at the end of the stack in canonical order.
    assert services[-5:] == [
        "frei0r.pixeliz0r",
        "frei0r.glitch0r",
        "frei0r.rgbsplit0r",
        "frei0r.scanline0r",
        "avfilter.exposure",
    ], services

    # The preset takes one explicit snapshot; `serialize_project` takes
    # one additional pre-write snapshot. The critical invariant is that the
    # preset itself only calls `create_snapshot` once (not 5 times, once per
    # filter), so the delta is exactly 2.
    assert _count_snapshots(ws) - before == 2
    snap_dir = ws / "projects" / "snapshots" / out["data"]["snapshot_id"]
    assert snap_dir.is_dir()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SR-14: intensity scales per-filter params
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_sr14_glitch_stack_intensity_scales_params(tmp_path):
    ws_low, pf_low = _make_ws(tmp_path / "low")
    ws_high, pf_high = _make_ws(tmp_path / "high")

    out_low = effect_glitch_stack(
        workspace_path=str(ws_low), project_file=pf_low,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP, intensity=0.0,
    )
    out_high = effect_glitch_stack(
        workspace_path=str(ws_high), project_file=pf_high,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP, intensity=1.0,
    )
    assert out_low["status"] == "success"
    assert out_high["status"] == "success"

    low_filters = _list_filters(ws_low, pf_low, TRACK, CLIP)
    high_filters = _list_filters(ws_high, pf_high, TRACK, CLIP)

    # pixeliz0r block width (property "0") must differ

# SR-17: effect_fade inserts transform with keyframed rect
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_sr17_fade_writes_opacity_keyframes_on_transform(tmp_path):
    ws, pf = _make_ws(tmp_path)
    before = _count_snapshots(ws)

    out = effect_fade(
        workspace_path=str(ws), project_file=pf,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP,
        fade_in_frames=30, fade_out_frames=30,
        easing="ease_in_out",
    )
    assert out["status"] == "success", out
    assert 2 <= out["data"]["keyframe_count"] <= 4

    filters = _list_filters(ws, pf, TRACK, CLIP)
    # Find the newly-added affine+transform filter at end of stack.
    last = filters[-1]
    assert last["mlt_service"] == "affine"
    assert last["kdenlive_id"] == "transform"
    rect = last["properties"].get("rect", "")
    # Should contain keyframe separators (';') with operator-encoded opacity.
    assert ";" in rect, rect
    # Contains an opacity term '0' and '1'.
    assert " 0" in rect or "=0" in rect
    assert " 1" in rect or "=1" in rect

    # One explicit + one serializer-level pre-write snapshot.
    assert _count_snapshots(ws) - before == 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SR-18: fade zero/zero errors
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_sr18_fade_zero_zero_errors(tmp_path):
    ws, pf = _make_ws(tmp_path)
    out = effect_fade(
        workspace_path=str(ws), project_file=pf,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP,
        fade_in_frames=0, fade_out_frames=0,
    )
    assert out["status"] == "error"
    assert "non-zero" in out["message"] or "at least one" in out["message"]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SR-19: fade honors easing -- MLT operator char in keyframe string
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_sr19_fade_easing_mlt_operator_matches(tmp_path):
    from workshop_video_brain.edit_mcp.pipelines.keyframes import resolve_easing

    ws, pf = _make_ws(tmp_path)
    op = resolve_easing("ease_in_out")
    out = effect_fade(
        workspace_path=str(ws), project_file=pf,
        track=TRACK, clip=CLIP,
        fade_in_frames=30, fade_out_frames=30,
        easing="ease_in_out",
    )
    assert out["status"] == "success", out

    filters = _list_filters(ws, pf, TRACK, CLIP)
    rect = filters[-1]["properties"].get("rect", "")
    # resolve_easing("ease_in_out") -> cubic_in_out family default operator 'i'.
    # We look for the op char before '=' at least once.
    assert f"{op}=" in rect, f"expected op {op!r} in {rect!r}"
